entity.summary: Ethereum-born decentralized exchange protocol focused on stablecoin and correlated-asset trading. Curve launched in 2020, later expanded across multiple chains and products, and remained active after a July 2023 Vyper-related exploit affected several pools.
entity.notes: Entity-level umbrella record. The launch day is stored at first-of-month precision because the strongest official retrospective source supports the 2020 launch year rather than a single exact public launch date. Pool deployments and chain-specific instances are not split in HEI v0.

events.title: Curve Finance launched as a stablecoin-focused AMM
events.description: Curve began operating in 2020 as an automated market maker designed for efficient swaps between stablecoins and other closely correlated assets. HEI records the launch at month precision because available official retrospective material establishes the year but not a single exact launch day.
events.notes: The stored date is a precision placeholder for January 2020, not a claim that the protocol launched exactly at midnight on January 1.

events.title: Vyper reentrancy vulnerability affected multiple Curve pools
events.description: Several Curve pools were exploited on 2023-07-30 after a vulnerability in specific Vyper compiler versions enabled reentrancy attacks. The incident affected pools including alETH/ETH, pETH/ETH and JPEG-related pools, but Curve continued operating and later pursued community-funded reimbursement proposals.
events.notes: The incident did not end the Curve protocol; status remains active.

evidence.title: Curve Finance: The Rise of the Home of Stablecoins
evidence.notes: Official retrospective describing Curve's development from its 2020 launch and later DAO and product milestones.

evidence.title: Curve Finance official application
evidence.notes: Current official application confirming continued operation under the Curve Finance brand.

evidence.title: Proposal to Recompensate LPs Affected by Curve Pool Exploit
evidence.notes: Primary governance record identifying pools and liquidity providers affected by the July 2023 exploit and proposing reimbursement.

evidence.title: Reimbursing costs incurred to JPEG’d DAO during the July exploit
evidence.notes: Primary governance discussion confirming the 2023-07-30 exploit and affected JPEG-related pools.

evidence.title: Curve Finance archived homepage index
evidence.notes: Archive index retained for historical website continuity.
